title: An Angel Dominated World
wikitext:
[]: An Angel Dominated World?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Thu Jan 10, 2013 11:33 pm Now, from what little could be interpreted from our forums, apparently the goal of the Angels series was to try to unite themselves with Adam (or Lilith) in order to cause their version of Third Impact. From what we can tell, their version would succeed exterminating all forms of Lilith-Based life on the planet, while at the same time allowing the propagation of their race. A sort of "Genocide-Genesis", if you'd call it that. So, if this were the case, what if the angels succeeded? What if humanity had failed in this prevention, and all life on earth was replaced by the angels? Or better yet, what if Lilith never landed on Earth? The angels themselves possess the "Fruit of Life", which bestows them with both Immortality and an immense variety of powers, as well as a huge myriad of forms. As such, what kind of biosphere would emerge from the dominance of these Eldritch Beings? What would happen to the Earth in order for these beings to be sustained?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Fri Jan 11, 2013 12:23 am Well, we could see SI as a small-scale reset - Fuyutsuki mentions a "xhange of atmospheric components above a perfectly defined area" as one of the fishier things about SI. That said, Lillithian life also "brought" its own atmosphere - The current composition, especially the oxygen have only been that way since the first photosynthetic bacteria came around, so maybe it's something similar for the angels?

[]: Re: An Angel Dominated World?             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 12:36 am xanderkh wrote: What would happen to the Earth in order for these beings to be sustained?

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:25 am Why should they have to move at all? It was supposed to be their planet, even Seele concedes this.

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:51 am Why? Because the Angels culminate in Kaworu, the very embodiment of unselfishness. The dude just goes away to some far away star together with his sons in his NGE2's scenario, couldn't they just move to some close planet and find a way to coexist? The answer is no, because the [s:2w35bv4a]plot[/s:2w35bv4a] Anno said so, obviously...

[]: Re: An Angel Dominated World?             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Jan 11, 2013 1:56 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Did they need to be sustained by Earth in the first place? The Angels apparently don't necessarily need to live inside an atmosphere (look at Arael and Sahaquiel), nor they seem to care a lot about extreme temperatures (Sandalphon), it always puzzled me why they needed Earth in the first place, couldn't they just move to the Moon or Mars? Well, I guess that this is partially a moot point because they needed Adam in the first place to restart their Genesis and humans neither were capable (probably) nor were willing (because of Seele's plans) to "assist" them.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Fri Jan 11, 2013 2:06 am Hyper Shinchan wrote: Why? Because the Angels culminate in Kaworu, the very embodiment of unselfishness. The dude just goes away to some far away star together with his sons in his NGE2's scenario, couldn't they just move to some close planet and find a way to coexist? The answer is no, because the [s]plot[/s] Anno said so, obviously...

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 2:46 am Kaworu has a privileged view of things anyways. The other Angels may not have known anything about the FAR and their intentions while Kaworu does because he was one. In the end he gave the planet to us over his children but his motivations needn't apply to the rest of them.

[]:             Quote Post by A.T. Fish » Fri Jan 11, 2013 6:28 am If the angels were the dominant race I'm pretty sure they'd have colonized the other planets in the solar system by now. I can see the angels organizing into a social structure reminiscent of the Zerg colonies.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Jan 11, 2013 3:46 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Why should they have to move at all? It was supposed to be their planet, even Seele concedes this.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Jan 11, 2013 4:16 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Because they can and we can't. It's not our fault their mom put herself to sleep for Lilith's sake.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Fri Jan 11, 2013 6:10 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You realize the Angels are all the same shapeshifting species, right? Sahaquiel took that form for the sake of dealing with us, it's not the form he's born with or stuck in.

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Fri Jan 11, 2013 7:14 pm xanderkh wrote: Then, by that standard, what would be the point of their existence? Being granted with the Fruit of Life, they're bestowed with Immortality, god-like powers, and the ability to shapeshift to adapt to any situation and environment. They are the perfect species to exist and survive at the top of the chain of life....but then what?

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Fri Jan 11, 2013 10:08 pm AuraTwilight wrote: You realize the Angels are all the same shapeshifting species, right?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 1:39 am Here's the previous iteration of this thread. AuraTwilight wrote: Because they can and we can't. It's not our fault their mom put herself to sleep for Lilith's sake.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Sat Jan 12, 2013 2:54 am AuraTwilight wrote: Because they can and we can't. It's not our fault their mom put herself to sleep for Lilith's sake.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 5:14 am Agentomega wrote: I suspect the Angels are at least bound to some set of laws that govern their forms though.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sat Jan 12, 2013 6:04 am xanderkh wrote: Then, by that standard, what would be the point of their existence? Being granted with the Fruit of Life, they're bestowed with Immortality, god-like powers, and the ability to shapeshift to adapt to any situation and environment. They are the perfect species to exist and survive at the top of the chain of life....but then what?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Jan 12, 2013 7:42 am xanderkh wrote: Then, by that standard, what would be the point of their existence? Being granted with the Fruit of Life, they're bestowed with Immortality, god-like powers, and the ability to shapeshift to adapt to any situation and environment. They are the perfect species to exist and survive at the top of the chain of life....but then what?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Jan 12, 2013 11:54 am Kendrix wrote: So, rather than to exist as many forms that would die and reproduce, angel "evolution" would consist of one angel continously improving *itself*
